task_completion.description: Open the Subsurface application with the dive log loaded.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Subsurface application window is visible, displaying a list of dives and trips.

task_completion.description: Locate and select Dive #4 under the 'Sund Rock' trip.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Dive #4 is visible in the dive list and is highlighted or clicked. It should initially appear indented under a trip header named 'Sund Rock'.

task_completion.description: Remove Dive #4 from its trip grouping.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A context menu showing 'Remove dive from trip' is clicked for Dive #4, OR Dive #4 is subsequently shown as a standalone entry in the dive list (no longer indented under the 'Sund Rock' trip header).

task_completion.description: Locate and select Dive #8 under the 'Yellow House' trip.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Dive #8 is visible in the dive list and is highlighted or clicked. It should initially appear indented under a trip header named 'Yellow House'.

task_completion.description: Remove Dive #8 from its trip grouping.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A context menu showing 'Remove dive from trip' is clicked for Dive #8, OR Dive #8 is subsequently shown as a standalone entry in the dive list (no longer indented under the 'Yellow House' trip header).

task_completion.description: Save the updated dive log to the target file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Save' or 'Save As' option is clicked in the File menu, a save dialog targeting '/home/ga/Documents/dives.ssrf' is visible, or the main window title indicates there are no unsaved changes.

integrity.description: The agent used the Subsurface graphical user interface to modify the dive log.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Subsurface application window (menus, dive list, right-click context menus) rather than a terminal, script, or text editor modifying the .ssrf XML file directly.

integrity.description: The agent only removed the specified dives, leaving other trip groupings intact.
integrity.visual_evidence: Other dives in the list (such as Dive #1 or Dive #5) remain indented under their respective trip headers, proving the agent did not simply use a global 'flatten' or 'remove all trips' command.

integrity.description: The application state reflects genuine interaction with the loaded logbook.
integrity.visual_evidence: The dive list shows a realistic sequence of dives (up to 29 dives exist in the file) with correct trip headers ('Sund Rock', 'Yellow House') rather than mocked or manually typed text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target file is /home/ga/Documents/dives.ssrf. The task requires removing Dive #4 (chronological index 3) from the 'Sund Rock' trip and Dive #8 (chronological index 7) from the 'Yellow House' trip. Dives #1 (index 0) and #5 (index 4) must remain grouped in their respective trips. Verify that Dive #4 and Dive #8 appear as standalone, un-indented entries in the Subsurface dive list, while other dives remain indented under trip headers.
